WorkaduMCP
Features Setup Guide GitHub
v1.0.0 is live

Bring Your Workadu Data
to the AI Era

Seamlessly integrate your CRM, Bookings, and Invoices with Claude, Cursor, and other AI assistants using the Model Context Protocol.

Get Started View Source

38 Powerful Tools

Give your AI agent complete access to your Workadu workspace.

📦

Orders & Bookings

List, create, update, and cancel bookings. Automatically check availability and send email confirmations.

👥

Customers

Search through your CRM. Create new customer records or update existing ones seamlessly.

🧾

Invoices & AADE

Draft invoices, add line items, manage withholdings, and publish directly to the Greek tax authority (AADE).

🛎️

Services

Manage your products and services catalog. Read descriptions, prices, and update availability.

💰

Payments

Track financial transactions, create new payment records, and link them to orders and invoices.

🏭

Assets (DCL)

Manage physical assets and track their movements (dispatch notes, transfers) via the Digital Consignment Ledger.

Setup Guide

Connect your AI assistant in minutes. Choose your environment.

1. Clone & Build

Run this locally on your machine to start the stdio server.

git clone https://github.com/workadu/mcp-server.git
cd mcp-server
npm install
npm run build

2. Configure your AI Client

Add this to your Claude Desktop config (claude_desktop_config.json) or Cursor config (.cursor/mcp.json).

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "workadu": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/mcp-server/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "WORKADU_API_URL": "https://your-app.workadu.com",
        "WORKADU_API_KEY": "your-api-key-here"
      }
    }
  }
}

1. Use the Remote Server

The server is already hosted on Fly.io and supports SSE (Server-Sent Events) over HTTP.

Endpoint: https://mcp-server-tepc4a.fly.dev/sse

2. Configure your AI Client

Add this to your Claude Desktop config. The remote server uses headers to authenticate per-session.

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "workadu": {
      "url": "https://mcp-server-tepc4a.fly.dev/sse",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer your-api-key-here",
        "X-Workadu-Api-Url": "https://your-app.workadu.com"
      }
    }
  }
}

⚠️ Important Note

When using the remote server, your Workadu instance must be accessible from the public internet. Local URLs (like http://workadu.local) will not work unless tunneled via ngrok.
